query: consolidated literature-survey research-internet queries: see results section for individual queries

results.title: FrontierMath: A Benchmark for Evaluating Advanced Mathematical Reasoning in AI
results.snippet: FrontierMath benchmark of hundreds of original mathematics problems crafted by expert mathematicians; SOTA solves under 2%.

results.title: WorkArena: How Capable Are Web Agents at Solving Common Knowledge Work Tasks?
results.snippet: 33 web tasks on ServiceNow with BrowserGym; GPT-4 reaches 42.7%.

results.title: WorkArena++: Towards Compositional Planning and Reasoning-based Common Knowledge Work Tasks
results.snippet: 682 compositional tasks built from WorkArena atomics; NeurIPS 2024 D&B.
results.relevance_note: The exact benchmark named in project description.

results.title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-world Github Issues?
results.snippet: 2,294 GitHub issues across 12 Python repos; ICLR 2024 oral.

results.title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified
results.snippet: Human-validated 500-instance subset by OpenAI in collaboration with the SWE-bench team.

results.title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
results.snippet: Two domains (retail, airline); pass^k metric; gpt-4o under 50% pass@1.

results.title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in Language Models
results.snippet: Interleaves reasoning traces and actions; 34% gain on ALFWorld.

results.title: Plan-and-Solve Prompting
results.snippet: Two-step prompting: plan subtasks then solve; reduces missing-step errors in Zero-shot-CoT.
results.relevance_note: Direct example of granularity conditioning at the prompt level.

results.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
results.relevance_note: Hierarchical agent paper relevant to scope conditioning across episodes.

results.title: Least-to-Most Prompting Enables Complex Reasoning in Large Language Models
results.snippet: Decomposes problems into ordered subproblems; SCAN length-split 99%+.
results.relevance_note: Hierarchical decomposition prior with explicit difficulty granularity.

results.title: Can LLMs Express Their Uncertainty? An Empirical Evaluation of Confidence Elicitation in LLMs
results.snippet: Verbalized confidence elicitation framework; ICLR 2024; LLMs are overconfident.
results.relevance_note: Primary calibration / overconfidence reference for Metric 2.

results.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
results.relevance_note: Establishes baseline that subtask-granularity prompts compete against.
